gap_classification.description: Offline-first resilience not typically prioritized in EdTech blueprints, but empirically the #1 blocker (TB-4 severity: 1687.12).
gap_classification.recommendation: Offline-first architecture must be a P0 foundational constraint in SR system design.

gap_classification.description: Curriculum placement / prerequisite graph absent from standard adaptive learning designs, but TB-7 (severity: 668.44) confirms structural instructional mismatch is second-highest blocker.
gap_classification.recommendation: Mandatory diagnostic onboarding + prerequisite graph must precede any content delivery.

gap_classification.description: Gamification and engagement systems are often positioned early in EdTech blueprints, but empirically rank P2 (TB-2 severity: 51.93 vs TB-4 severity: 1687.12). Risk of overbuilding engagement before stability.
gap_classification.recommendation: Defer gamification to Phase 2 of SR implementation, after system stability and curriculum alignment.

gap_classification.description: Equity and affordability features (TB-6, severity: 86.47) are often treated as policy decisions, not system features. Empirical data confirms they block active users.
gap_classification.recommendation: Freemium pathways and effort-based unlocks should be system architecture concerns, not post-launch considerations.

gap_classification.description: AI Tutor (TB-3) correctly positioned as P2. Empirical data confirms users request support systems, but severity is not crisis-level (47.71). Building AI Mentor after stability layer is empirically justified.
gap_classification.recommendation: AI Tutor integration order is empirically validated. Proceed as planned.

gap_classification.description: TB-1 Cognitive Difficulty shows high frequency (2,591 docs) but low negativity (3.3%). A naive gap analysis might deprioritize cognitive features entirely. In reality, the positive majority signals the PLATFORM CAN SUCCEED at cognition — but the 3.3% are at-risk users needing support.
gap_classification.recommendation: Design cognitive adaptation features for the minority at-risk learners, not as a platform-wide remediation.
